title: Tigers Crush Sri Lanka by 7 Wickets in Colombo Test Series Opener
summary: Bangladesh's pace attack dismantled the Sri Lankan top order in just 34 overs, with Taskin Ahmed claiming five wickets on a rain-affected pitch.
content:
Bangladesh's pace attack delivered a commanding performance in Colombo on Sunday, dismissing Sri Lanka for just 134 runs in their first innings and then chasing down a modest 142-run target with seven wickets and more than a session to spare.

Taskin Ahmed was the standout performer, claiming five wickets for 38 runs to dismantle the Sri Lankan top order on a pitch that offered more assistance to pace bowlers than the hosts had anticipated.

"I've been working hard on my length and the seam position," Taskin said in the post-match press conference. "The pitch was helpful but you still have to execute, and today the team executed as a unit."

Bangladesh's reply was anchored by a composed half-century from Najmul Hossain Shanto, who brought up his fifty off 74 balls before falling to an edge behind the wicket. Mominul Haque and Mushfiqur Rahim then guided the Tigers to their target without further alarm.

title: FC Barcelona Signs Teenage Prodigy in Record-Breaking 180M Euro Deal
summary: The Catalan giants break the world transfer record as they secure the Argentine wunderkind amid fierce competition from Premier League clubs.
content:
FC Barcelona shattered the world transfer record on Saturday by confirming the signing of 18-year-old Argentine forward Mateo Ibarra from River Plate in a deal worth 180 million euros, eclipsing the previous record set by Paris Saint-Germain's acquisition of Neymar in 2017.

The deal, which had been the subject of frenzied speculation for weeks, was concluded after Barcelona president Joan Laporta personally flew to Buenos Aires to finalize terms with the player's representatives and River Plate officials.

Manchester City and Liverpool had both been heavily linked with Ibarra, with reports suggesting that City had offered a higher base fee but that the player's preference for Barcelona ultimately proved decisive.

Ibarra is expected to be officially unveiled at the Camp Nou on Monday and could make his debut in Barcelona's La Liga fixture against Atletico Madrid the following weekend.

title: Shakib Al Hasan Named Bangladesh ODI Captain for World Cup Campaign
summary: The board's decision ends weeks of speculation, with the veteran all-rounder set to lead a squad featuring several promising newcomers.
content:
The Bangladesh Cricket Board announced on Wednesday that Shakib Al Hasan would captain the national One Day International side for the upcoming ICC Cricket World Cup qualifying campaign, ending weeks of uncertainty that had surrounded the team's leadership.

BCB President Faruque Ahmed confirmed the appointment at a press conference at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium, describing Shakib as the natural leader for a campaign of this magnitude given his unparalleled experience in global tournaments.

The squad announced alongside the captaincy appointment features five players who have fewer than ten ODI appearances, reflecting the BCB's stated intention to use the qualifying campaign to blood younger talent alongside proven performers.

Shakib, speaking briefly after the announcement, said the team's goal was not merely qualification but performing in a way that would build momentum heading into the tournament itself.
